frame_analyses.description: Initial setup showing empty laparoscopic trainer with Penrose drain visible on suture block
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean starting position with drain properly positioned

frame_analyses.description: First instrument enters field, timing begins
Loading needle with suture, instruments positioning for first pass
Suture placed through drain, instruments working to position for knot tying
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture appears well placed through both marks on drain

frame_analyses.description: Both instruments manipulating suture ends, preparing for first throw
Creating surgeon's knot - double throw being formed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper surgeon's knot technique with double wrap visible

frame_analyses.description: Completing and tightening first throw with instruments
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good tension control during knot tightening

frame_analyses.description: Beginning second throw with hand switching
Executing final single throw with appropriate hand switching
Final knot tightening, drain appearing well approximated
Cutting first suture end with green-handled scissors
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper cutting technique close to knot

frame_analyses.description: Task completed, both suture ends cut, drain well approximated

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double throw executed well
Good hand switching and single throw technique
Final throw with proper hand switching, knot appears very secure

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain edges well approximated with no visible gap, excellent closure

technique_summary: Excellent performance with proper surgeon's knot, appropriate hand switching between throws, and precise suture placement. The trainee demonstrates significant improvement in speed and technique consistency.
improvement_suggestions: Consider slightly faster needle loading to optimize time
Continue practicing to maintain this level of precision
strengths: Improved completion time showing good progression
